mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits an easier discount-rate channel to equities, raising the present value of future cash flows and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when policy is actively easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in more than 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 2-year rank correlation stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE signals weakening household income and demand, which depresses earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days once unemployment starts moving higher.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes deliver positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 standard error.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rebound in RSAFS and GDPC1 indicates broad demand recovery, improving revenue growth expectations and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint increase in RSAFS and GDPC1 does not outperform either series alone in rank IC, or if 60-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of joint-recovery observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in INDPRO captures industrial shutdown and operating leverage stress, which lowers earnings revisions and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls by more than 2.0% over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not negative in at least 60% of large INDPRO decline episodes, or if the signal's rolling 3-year hit rate falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST rises while UNRATE remains elevated, markets infer stabilizing housing demand without a full labor recovery, which supports a short-term relief rally in the S&P 500 over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises month-over-month while UNRATE stays above 5.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition fails to produce positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the interaction term is not materially better than each single-series effect.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation surge combined with a near-zero FEDFUNDS rate indicates negative real policy rates and stronger liquidity support, which lifts equity multiples and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation is positive while FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-low-rate condition does not raise the 20-day SPX hit rate above 55%, or if positive returns disappear once either proxy is controlled for separately.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS after a prior employment peak signals payroll contraction and weaker consumer spending, which leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS is below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative in more than 55% of payroll-contraction periods, or if the signal loses significance after accounting for its own rolling mean.

mechanism_narrative: In a severe demand shock, simultaneous weakness in GDPC1 and RSAFS identifies collapsing final demand, and equities continue falling over the next month as revenue revisions outrun policy support.
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS do not correspond to negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of observations, or if the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero.
